dataFolderName.description: Data folder name for per-variant isolation. Controls both the Halo config directory (~/.{dataFolderName}/) and Electron userData directory. Defaults to 'halo' when omitted.

url.description: URL for generic provider (empty string = disabled)

owner.description: GitHub repository owner (required for github provider)

repo.description: GitHub repository name (required for github provider)

authProviders.description: Authentication provider configurations. An entry can be one of three forms: (1) builtin=true for in-tree providers (e.g. custom), (2) path=... for dynamic OAuth provider modules, (3) preset=... for a fixed-baseUrl API-key form (mutually exclusive with `path`). The `type` field is a free-form identifier; the special value 'preset-api' is recommended for preset entries but the renderer routes purely on the presence of `preset`.

preset.description: Preset API configuration. When present, this provider renders as an API-key form with a fixed baseUrl. Mutually exclusive with `path` (dynamic OAuth providers).

baseUrl.description: Fixed base URL of the gateway (no path suffix). e.g. 'http://example.com/public'

apiType.description: Persisted to AISource.apiType. Determines dispatch path for Anthropic/Halo SDK engines. Codex engine ignores this and uses OpenAI protocol.

modelsPath.description: Path appended to baseUrl for GET model list. Default: '/v1/models'. The endpoint must return OpenAI-compatible { data: [{ id }] }.

fallbackModels.description: Used when GET models fails or returns empty.

docs.description: Optional documentation link shown in the form.

label.description: Localized text. Defaults to 'Learn more' / '了解更多' if absent.

browserPolicy.description: Browser network access policy. Controls which domains the embedded browser can navigate to. Omit for unrestricted access (default).

mode.description: Policy mode: allowlist = only listed domains allowed, blocklist = listed domains blocked, unrestricted = no restrictions

allowlist.description: Domain patterns to allow (e.g. "*.example.com", "intranet.corp.com"). Used when mode is "allowlist".

blocklist.description: Domain patterns to block. Used when mode is "blocklist".

homepage.description: Default homepage URL for new browser tabs. Falls back to about:blank when policy is active but homepage is not set.

telemetry.description: Telemetry configuration (enterprise/custom builds only). Controls which user-authored / user-identifiable fields may be forwarded to the self-hosted telemetry backend. Omit for open-source builds — every sensitive field is dropped by default.

allowedSensitiveFields.description: Whitelist of SENSITIVE_KEYS property names permitted to be sent. Recognized keys: specId, spaceName, modelName, sourceName, mcpId, skillId, imBotName, inputTokens, outputTokens, errorCode.

security.description: Security policy for enterprise/custom builds. Each flag follows the '<surface>Safe' convention: true enables restrictions, omitted/false keeps the permissive default. Open-source builds omit this entirely. Flag definitions live in src/main/services/security-policy.ts.

remoteMcpSafe.description: When true, every remote HTTP endpoint that could land an MCP server configuration (AppSpec type=mcp, mcp_server patch, mcpServers map, store install of MCP type) returns 403. Local UI install paths are unaffected. Use this to prevent remote callers from registering arbitrary native commands.

credentialAtRestSafe.description: When true, the stored remote-access credential is encrypted at rest with SM4-CBC plus HMAC-SM3 (encrypt-then-MAC; GM/T 0002 + GM/T 0091) under a machine-bound KEK. When omitted/false (open-source default), the credential is stored as plain text. The plaintext value is held in memory in both modes so the UI can keep displaying the current PIN; login validation uses crypto.timingSafeEqual against the in-memory value.

tunnelSafe.description: When true, the Cloudflare Quick Tunnel feature is disabled at every layer: the service refuses to spawn cloudflared, the remote:tunnel:enable IPC short-circuits before reaching the service, and the renderer hides the 'Internet Access' subsection plus the Cloudflared row in System Diagnostics. Use this when policy forbids outbound HTTPS tunnels that bypass the corporate proxy.

registryOverrides.description: Per-built-in-registry override map (enterprise/custom builds only). Keys are built-in registry IDs ('official', 'mcp-official', 'smithery', 'claude-skills', 'skillhub'). Each entry is merged on top of the built-in defaults during ensureBuiltinRegistries() on every startup. Omit any field to keep the built-in default. Open-source builds omit this entirely.

additionalProperties.description: Override for one built-in registry. Only declared fields are applied; omitted fields retain their built-in defaults.

url.description: Replace the registry endpoint (e.g. point to an internal mirror).

name.description: Replace the display name shown in the Store UI.

enabled.description: Force-enable or force-disable the registry on every startup; overrides the user's manual toggle. The entry remains visible in the Store UI, just locked.

hidden.description: When true, the registry is removed entirely from the Store UI and any persisted copy from a previous run is dropped on the next startup. Takes precedence over `enabled`. Use this — not `enabled: false` — when policy forbids the registry outright.

publish.description: Publish target. Determines where the App detail 'Publish' button routes the spec.

builtinApps.description: Built-in digital humans bundled with the build (VSCode-style built-in extensions). Each entry is materialized into resources/builtin-apps/<specId>/ at build time by scripts/sync-builtin-apps.mjs and auto-installed on first launch by the App Manager. Open-source builds typically omit this field; enterprise builds (e.g. product.<variant>.json) declare their own list pointing to a private SSOT repository.

sourcePath.description: Path to the external SSOT directory containing each built-in app folder. Resolved relative to product.json's directory (i.e. project root). Each entry's specId must match a subdirectory containing spec.yaml and an optional skills/ directory. May be overridden at build time via the HALO_BUILTIN_APPS_SOURCE environment variable.

apps.description: List of built-in apps to bundle. The order has no functional meaning.

specId.description: Subdirectory name inside sourcePath. Becomes the canonical spec identifier.

spaceId.description: Target space for installation. Use 'halo-temp' for the default temp space, or null for global scope. Defaults to 'halo-temp'.

defaultStatus.description: Initial status when first installed. Use 'paused' for apps that require user configuration or browser login before they can run safely. Defaults to 'paused'.
